arguments.content:
# Friar

## Class Summary

The **Friar** is a full divine caster who crushes the unclean with relic-bearing zeal, sustains the party with potent healing, and turns the dead through the authority of Solace.

### Core Identity
- **Primary Attributes:** Piety
- **Level 10 Title:** Saint of the Last Rite
- **Hit Die:** d8
- **Armor:** Any armor
- **Shield:** Can equip shield
- **Weapon Proficiencies:** All bludgeoning weapons
- **Spellcasting:** Full caster using **Piety**
- **Special Role:** Full divine caster, undead controller, healer, quarterstaff battle-priest

---

## Level Progression

| Level | Title | Feature / Growth Highlights |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Penitent Wanderer | Spellcasting (Piety), Quarterstaff Mastery |
| 2 | Ashen Disciple | Turn Undead |
| 3 | Flagellant | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 4 | Keeper of Relics | Sacred Retribution |
| 5 | Lantern Bearer | Thunderstrike, Empowered Healing |
| 6 | Voice of Penance | Turn Undead (Flee), Sanctified Staff |
| 7 | Mortified Saint | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 8 | Warden of Ash | Divine Restoration |
| 9 | Living Reliquary | Embrace of Solace |
| 10 | Saint of the Last Rite | Turn Undead (Annihilation), Uncapped Attribute Growth |

---

## Features

### Spellcasting
You can cast spells as a **full caster**.

Rules:
- the Friar uses **Piety** as its spellcasting attribute
- spell access and spell recovery follow Umberhold's current spellcasting rules
- at character creation, a Friar gains all currently approved **F cantrips** and **F 1st-level spells**

---

### Quarterstaff Mastery
Your preferred weapon is the **quarterstaff**.

While wielding a quarterstaff:
- your attacks score a **critical hit on 19-20**
- on a critical hit, the target is **Stunned** until the end of its next turn

---

### Turn Undead
Requires a **holy symbol of Solace**.

As an action, all undead must make a **Piety saving throw** against your **spell DC**.

On a failure:
- they gain the **Frightened** condition
- they may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns

Rules:
- this ability may be used **once per combat**
- it may be used up to **three times before requiring a Rest**
- if an undead creature rolls a **natural 1** on this saving throw, it is **destroyed**

---

### Uncapped Attribute Growth
You can increase:
- one attribute by **2**
- or two attributes by **1** each

---

### Sacred Retribution
When an **undead creature** hits you with a **melee attack**, you may immediately make a melee attack against that creature, provided:
- it is within your reach
- you are wielding a melee weapon

Rules:
- this effect can occur only **once per turn**

---

### Thunderstrike
When you hit with a melee attack, there is a chance that Solace's wrath calls down a bolt of lightning upon your target.

On a successful trigger:
- the target takes an additional **4d6 lightning** damage

### Formula
```text
15% + 1% per point of your Luck modifier
```

---

### Empowered Healing
Your healing spells restore **one additional healing die**, matching the spell's normal healing die.

---

### Turn Undead (Flee)
Undead that fail their saving throw now **flee**.

Rules:
- your party gains **full experience** as if they were slain
- they drop **no treasure**

---

### Sanctified Staff
Your **quarterstaff** attacks deal **one additional die of holy damage**.

---

### Divine Restoration
Your healing spells can now **critically heal**.

When you cast a healing spell, roll a **d20**.
On a result of **20**:
- the healing is **doubled** as per a critical hit

---

### Embrace of Solace
Damage that would reduce you to **0 hit points** instead reduces you to **1 hit point**.

Rules:
- this effect may occur **once**
- it recharges after a **Rest**

---

### Turn Undead (Annihilation)
Undead that fail their saving throw are **destroyed**.

---

## Notes

### Current Role
The Friar is currently defined as:
- full divine caster
- primary healer
- anti-undead controller and destroyer
- quarterstaff-focused battle-priest
- resilient holy support class

### Current Spell Access
Under current Umberhold spellcasting canon, the Friar begins with:
- all approved **F cantrips**
- all approved **F 1st-level spells**

The current early spell list includes:
- **Deathknell of Solace**
- **Cure Minor Wounds**
- **Judgment of Solace**
- **Litany of Solace**
- **Shield of Solace**
- **Solace's Blessing**

### Current Open Mechanical Questions
These may need later clarification:
- how **Turn Undead** resolves against mixed enemy formations and whether all undead in both ranks are always affected
- whether **Turn Undead (Flee)** and **Turn Undead (Annihilation)** fully replace the base frightened result or add to it conditionally
- how **Sacred Retribution** is resolved in Umberhold's no-reaction combat model beyond its explicit once-per-turn trigger
- whether **Empowered Healing** applies to every healing spell uniformly, including rank-wide healing such as **Litany of Solace**
- whether **Divine Restoration** doubles only the final healing total or doubles all healing dice before modifiers

content.partialJson:
{"path":"umberhold/classes/friar.md","content":"# Friar

## Class Summary

The **Friar** is a full divine caster who crushes the unclean with relic-bearing zeal, sustains the party with potent healing, and turns the dead through the authority of Solace.

### Core Identity
- **Primary Attributes:** Piety
- **Level 10 Title:** Saint of the Last Rite
- **Hit Die:** d8
- **Armor:** Any armor
- **Shield:** Can equip shield
- **Weapon Proficiencies:** All bludgeoning weapons
- **Spellcasting:** Full caster using **Piety**
- **Special Role:** Full divine caster, undead controller, healer, quarterstaff battle-priest

---

## Level Progression

| Level | Title | Feature / Growth Highlights |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Penitent Wanderer | Spellcasting (Piety), Quarterstaff Mastery |
| 2 | Ashen Disciple | Turn Undead |
| 3 | Flagellant | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 4 | Keeper of Relics | Sacred Retribution |
| 5 | Lantern Bearer | Thunderstrike, Empowered Healing |
| 6 | Voice of Penance | Turn Undead (Flee), Sanctified Staff |
| 7 | Mortified Saint | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 8 | Warden of Ash | Divine Restoration |
| 9 | Living Reliquary | Embrace of Solace |
| 10 | Saint of the Last Rite | Turn Undead (Annihilation), Uncapped Attribute Growth |

---

## Features

### Spellcasting
You can cast spells as a **full caster**.

Rules:
- the Friar uses **Piety** as its spellcasting attribute
- spell access and spell recovery follow Umberhold's current spellcasting rules
- at character creation, a Friar gains all currently approved **F cantrips** and **F 1st-level spells**

---

### Quarterstaff Mastery
Your preferred weapon is the **quarterstaff**.

While wielding a quarterstaff:
- your attacks score a **critical hit on 19-20**
- on a critical hit, the target is **Stunned** until the end of its next turn

---

### Turn Undead
Requires a **holy symbol of Solace**.

As an action, all undead must make a **Piety saving throw** against your **spell DC**.

On a failure:
- they gain the **Frightened** condition
- they may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns

Rules:
- this ability may be used **once per combat**
- it may be used up to **three times before requiring a Rest**
- if an undead creature rolls a **natural 1** on this saving throw, it is **destroyed**

---

### Uncapped Attribute Growth
You can increase:
- one attribute by **2**
- or two attributes by **1** each

---

### Sacred Retribution
When an **undead creature** hits you with a **melee attack**, you may immediately make a melee attack against that creature, provided:
- it is within your reach
- you are wielding a melee weapon

Rules:
- this effect can occur only **once per turn**

---

### Thunderstrike
When you hit with a melee attack, there is a chance that Solace's wrath calls down a bolt of lightning upon your target.

On a successful trigger:
- the target takes an additional **4d6 lightning** damage

### Formula
```text
15% + 1% per point of your Luck modifier
```

---

### Empowered Healing
Your healing spells restore **one additional healing die**, matching the spell's normal healing die.

---

### Turn Undead (Flee)
Undead that fail their saving throw now **flee**.

Rules:
- your party gains **full experience** as if they were slain
- they drop **no treasure**

---

### Sanctified Staff
Your **quarterstaff** attacks deal **one additional die of holy damage**.

---

### Divine Restoration
Your healing spells can now **critically heal**.

When you cast a healing spell, roll a **d20**.
On a result of **20**:
- the healing is **doubled** as per a critical hit

---

### Embrace of Solace
Damage that would reduce you to **0 hit points** instead reduces you to **1 hit point**.

Rules:
- this effect may occur **once**
- it recharges after a **Rest**

---

### Turn Undead (Annihilation)
Undead that fail their saving throw are **destroyed**.

---

## Notes

### Current Role
The Friar is currently defined as:
- full divine caster
- primary healer
- anti-undead controller and destroyer
- quarterstaff-focused battle-priest
- resilient holy support class

### Current Spell Access
Under current Umberhold spellcasting canon, the Friar begins with:
- all approved **F cantrips**
- all approved **F 1st-level spells**

The current early spell list includes:
- **Deathknell of Solace**
- **Cure Minor Wounds**
- **Judgment of Solace**
- **Litany of Solace**
- **Shield of Solace**
- **Solace's Blessing**

### Current Open Mechanical Questions
These may need later clarification:
- how **Turn Undead** resolves against mixed enemy formations and whether all undead in both ranks are always affected
- whether **Turn Undead (Flee)** and **Turn Undead (Annihilation)** fully replace the base frightened result or add to it conditionally
- how **Sacred Retribution** is resolved in Umberhold's no-reaction combat model beyond its explicit once-per-turn trigger
- whether **Empowered Healing** applies to every healing spell uniformly, including rank-wide healing such as **Litany of Solace**
- whether **Divine Restoration** doubles only the final healing total or doubles all healing dice before modifiers
"}

arguments.oldText:
**Spell Reflection:** When a spell directly targets you, there is a 5% + 1% per point of your Luck modifier chance to reflect the spell back at the caster. This does not apply to area-of-effect spells or abilities that do not specifically target you.

\page

Crusader
arguments.newText:
**Spell Reflection:** When a spell directly targets you, there is a 5% + 1% per point of your Luck modifier chance to reflect the spell back at the caster. This does not apply to area-of-effect spells or abilities that do not specifically target you.

\page

Friar

| Level | Title | Feature / Growth Highlights |
| ----- | ----- | --------------------------- |
| 1 | Penitent Wanderer | Spellcasting (Piety), Quarterstaff Mastery |
| 2 | Ashen Disciple | Turn Undead |
| 3 | Flagellant | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 4 | Keeper of Relics | Sacred Retribution |
| 5 | Lantern Bearer | Thunderstrike, Empowered Healing |
| 6 | Voice of Penance | Turn Undead (Flee), Sanctified Staff |
| 7 | Mortified Saint | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 8 | Warden of Ash | Divine Restoration |
| 9 | Living Reliquary | Embrace of Solace |
| 10 | Saint of the Last Rite | Turn Undead (Annihilation), Uncapped Attribute Growth |

**Spellcasting:** You are a full caster. Your spellcasting ability is Piety.

**Quarterstaff Mastery:** Your preferred weapon is the quarterstaff. While wielding a quarterstaff, your attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19-20. On a critical hit, the target is stunned until the end of its next turn.

**Turn Undead:** Requires a holy symbol of Solace. As an action, all undead must make a Piety saving throw against your spell DC. On a failure, they gain the Frightened condition and may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns.

This ability may be used once per combat and up to three times before requiring a Rest.

If an undead creature rolls a natural 1 on this saving throw, it is destroyed.

**Uncapped Attribute Growth:** You can permanently increase two attributes by 1 point each, or increase one attribute by 2 points.

**Sacred Retribution:** When an undead creature hits you with a melee attack, you may immediately make a melee attack against that creature, provided it is within your reach and you are wielding a melee weapon. This effect can only occur once per turn.

**Thunderstrike:** When you hit with a melee attack, there is a 15% + 1% per point of your Luck modifier chance that Solace's wrath calls down a bolt of lightning upon your target, dealing an additional 4d6 lightning damage.

**Empowered Healing:** Your healing spells restore an additional die of healing.

**Turn Undead (Flee):** Undead that fail their saving throw now flee. You gain full experience as if they were slain, but they drop no treasure.

**Sanctified Staff:** Your quarterstaff attacks deal an additional die of holy damage.

**Divine Restoration:** Your healing spells can now critically heal. When you cast a healing spell, roll a d20; on a 20, the healing is doubled as per a critical hit.

**Embrace of Solace:** Damage that would reduce you to 0 hit points instead reduces you to 1 hit point. This effect can occur once and recharges after a Rest.

**Turn Undead (Annihilation):** Undead that fail their saving throw are destroyed.

\page

Crusader

content.text:
# Friar

## Class Summary

The **Friar** is a full divine caster who crushes the unclean with relic-bearing zeal, sustains the party with potent healing, and turns the dead through the authority of Solace.

### Core Identity
- **Primary Attributes:** Piety
- **Level 10 Title:** Saint of the Last Rite
- **Hit Die:** d8
- **Armor:** Any armor
- **Shield:** Can equip shield
- **Weapon Proficiencies:** All bludgeoning weapons
- **Spellcasting:** Full caster using **Piety**
- **Special Role:** Full divine caster, undead controller, healer, quarterstaff battle-priest

---

## Level Progression

| Level | Title | Feature / Growth Highlights |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Penitent Wanderer | Spellcasting (Piety), Quarterstaff Mastery |
| 2 | Ashen Disciple | Turn Undead |
| 3 | Flagellant | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 4 | Keeper of Relics | Sacred Retribution |
| 5 | Lantern Bearer | Thunderstrike, Empowered Healing |
| 6 | Voice of Penance | Turn Undead (Flee), Sanctified Staff |
| 7 | Mortified Saint | Uncapped Attribute Growth |
| 8 | Warden of Ash | Divine Restoration |
| 9 | Living Reliquary | Embrace of Solace |
| 10 | Saint of the Last Rite | Turn Undead (Annihilation), Uncapped Attribute Growth |

---

## Features

### Spellcasting
You can cast spells as a **full caster**.

Rules:
- the Friar uses **Piety** as its spellcasting attribute
- spell access and spell recovery follow Umberhold's current spellcasting rules
- at character creation, a Friar gains all currently approved **F cantrips** and **F 1st-level spells**

---

### Quarterstaff Mastery
Your preferred weapon is the **quarterstaff**.

While wielding a quarterstaff:
- your attacks score a **critical hit on 19-20**
- on a critical hit, the target is **Stunned** until the end of its next turn

---

### Turn Undead
Requires a **holy symbol of Solace**.

As an action, all undead must make a **Piety saving throw** against your **spell DC**.

On a failure:
- they gain the **Frightened** condition
- they may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns

Rules:
- this ability may be used **once per combat**
- it may be used up to **three times before requiring a Rest**
- if an undead creature rolls a **natural 1** on this saving throw, it is **destroyed**

---

### Uncapped Attribute Growth
You can increase:
- one attribute by **2**
- or two attributes by **1** each

---

### Sacred Retribution
When an **undead creature** hits you with a **melee attack**, you may immediately make a melee attack against that creature, provided:
- it is within your reach
- you are wielding a melee weapon

Rules:
- this effect can occur only **once per turn**

---

### Thunderstrike
When you hit with a melee attack, there is a chance that Solace's wrath calls down a bolt of lightning upon your target.

On a successful trigger:
- the target takes an additional **4d6 lightning** damage

### Formula
```text
15% + 1% per point of your Luck modifier
```

---

### Empowered Healing
Your healing spells restore **one additional healing die**, matching the spell's normal healing die.

---

### Turn Undead (Flee)
Undead that fail their saving throw now **flee**.

Rules:
- your party gains **full experience** as if they were slain
- they drop **no treasure**

---

### Sanctified Staff
Your **quarterstaff** attacks deal **one additional die of holy damage**.

---

### Divine Restoration
Your healing spells can now **critically heal**.

When you cast a healing spell, roll a **d20**.
On a result of **20**:
- the healing is **doubled** as per a critical hit

---

### Embrace of Solace
Damage that would reduce you to **0 hit points** instead reduces you to **1 hit point**.

Rules:
- this effect may occur **once**
- it recharges after a **Rest**

---

### Turn Undead (Annihilation)
Undead that fail their saving throw are **destroyed**.

---

## Notes

### Current Role
The Friar is currently defined as:
- full divine caster
- primary healer
- anti-undead controller and destroyer
- quarterstaff-focused battle-priest
- resilient holy support class

### Current Spell Access
Under current Umberhold spellcasting canon, the Friar begins with:
- all approved **F cantrips**
- all approved **F 1st-level spells**

The current early spell list includes:
- **Deathknell of Solace**
- **Cure Minor Wounds**
- **Judgment of Solace**
- **Litany of Solace**
- **Shield of Solace**
- **Solace's Blessing**

### Current Open Mechanical Questions
These may need later clarification:
- how **Turn Undead** resolves against mixed enemy formations and whether all undead in both ranks are always affected
- whether **Turn Undead (Flee)** and **Turn Undead (Annihilation)** fully replace the base frightened result or add to it conditionally
- how **Sacred Retribution** is resolved in Umberhold's no-reaction combat model beyond its explicit once-per-turn trigger
- whether **Empowered Healing** applies to every healing spell uniformly, including rank-wide healing such as **Litany of Solace**
- whether **Divine Restoration** doubles only the final healing total or doubles all healing dice before modifiers

# Umberhold Classes

This folder contains individual class entries for Umberhold.

---

## Class Index

| Class | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| [Shadowman](./shadowman.md) | Detailed | Stealth striker, trap specialist, and poison harvester |
| [Warden](./warden.md) | Detailed | Hunter-scout half caster using Agility and Piety |
| [Hedge Wizard](./hedge-wizard.md) | Detailed | Full caster using Intellect, elemental mastery, and anti-magic defenses |
| [Friar](./friar.md) | Detailed | Full caster using Piety with healing, quarterstaff mastery, and undead control |
| [Crusader](./crusader.md) | Detailed | Armored half caster using Piety, holy smites, and defensive support auras |
| [Troubadour](./troubadour.md) | Partial | Half caster using Personality with instrument-based songs and party support |

---

## Notes

- Class equipment rules are tracked in `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`.
- Spell access is tracked in `umberhold/class-spell-lists.md` and `umberhold/spellcasting-canon.md`.
- This folder is intended for one-file-per-class authoring.

# Classes Meta

Extracted from `umberhold/CURRENT.md`.

## Summary
`CURRENT.md` names **7 classes**.
At this point:
- **Shadowman** has a real 1-10 progression table and feature breakdown
- **Warden** now also has a detailed level progression and feature pass
- **Hedge Wizard** now also has a detailed level progression and feature pass
- **Crusader** now also has a detailed level progression and feature pass
- **Friar** now also has a detailed level progression and feature pass
- **Troubadour** now has a partial class file with its song list and support identity defined
- the remaining gaps are mostly in unfinished level progression and feature timing

---

## Classes Found

| Class | Primary Attributes | Level 10 Title | Current State in `CURRENT.md` |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shadowman | AGI / LCK | Dread-Shade | **Detailed** |
| Man-at-Arms | STR / CON | Warlord | Summary only |
| Warden | AGI / PIE | Nature-Wraith | **Detailed** |
| Hedge Wizard | INT | Black Archon | **Detailed** |
| Friar | PIE | Saint of the Last Rite | **Detailed** |
| Crusader | STR / PIE | Divine Exemplar | **Detailed** |
| Troubadour | PRE / LCK | Oracle | Partial; songs defined |

---

## What Is Already Defined

### Shadowman
Defined in meaningful detail:
- full level 1-10 title table
- core combat identity
- lock/trap specialist role
- stealth attack pattern
- poison harvesting identity
- several named class features

### Warden
Defined in meaningful detail:
- full level 1-10 title table
- half-caster identity using **PIE**
- ranged hunter-scout role
- beast-hunter and outdoors utility features
- late-game ranged control and execution features

### Hedge Wizard
Defined in meaningful detail:
- full level 1-10 title table
- full-caster identity using **INT**
- elemental specialization path
- charge-efficiency and anti-magic features
- late-game spell critical and spell reflection features

### Crusader
Defined in meaningful detail:
- full level 1-10 title table
- half-caster identity using **PIE**
- front-rank holy bruiser role
- anti-evil and anti-undead feature suite
- defensive aura and touch-healing support tools

### Friar
Defined in meaningful detail:
- full level 1-10 title table
- full-caster identity using **PIE**
- anti-undead control and destruction tools
- quarterstaff mastery and holy retaliation features
- strong healing and restoration identity

### Troubadour
Partially defined:
- core class identity using **PRE / LCK**
- half-caster identity using **Personality**
- support and song-based performance role
- current approved song list
- early spell access and instrument-based song action

### Other Incomplete Classes
These classes still exist mostly at the summary level:
- Man-at-Arms

For those classes, current data is still mainly:
- class name
- broad role
- primary stats
- level 10 title
- rough caster type where applicable

---

## What Still Needs To Be Authored

## Man-at-Arms
Needs:
- levels 1-10 title table
- level-by-level features
- weapon/armor identity
- tank mechanics
- defensive actions / taunt / guard rules if any
- initiative and survivability profile
- class progression text
- starting gear identity

## Warden
Current status:
- full levels 1-10 title table authored
- class features authored
- casting stat resolved to **PIE**
- ranged combat identity established
- hunt / scout mechanics established

Still needs:
- half-caster spell list expansion beyond currently approved early spells
- front-rank vs rear-rank role definition in final combat party guidance

- clarification on late-level interaction between Extra Attack, Death Shot, Concussive Shot, and Multishot

## Hedge Wizard
Current status:
- full levels 1-10 title table authored
- class features authored
- casting stat resolved to **INT**
- elemental / anti-magic identity established
- starting spell access now aligns with current spellcasting canon

Still needs:

## Friar

Still needs:

## Crusader
Current status:

Still needs:

## Troubadour
Current status:

Added and synced Friar.

Changed:
- `umberhold/classes/friar.md`
- `umberhold/classes/README.md`
- `umberhold/meta/classes.md`
- `umberhold/CURRENT.md`
- `umberhold/MASTER.md`

Included:
- full Friar 1–10 progression
- all listed Friar features
- d8 / any armor / shield / all bludgeoning weapons from canon
- full caster using **Piety**
- updated level 10 title to **Saint of the Last Rite**

Also updated metadata:
- Friar is now marked **Detailed**
- unfinished full-class tables are now down to **2**: `Man-at-Arms` and `Troubadour`

If you want, send the **Troubadour 1–10 level table** next and I’ll finish that class too.
